name: Sigrblót

timing.historical_anchor: First day of Harpa (The first month of Summer)
timing.lunar_logic: New Moon marking the start of the summer half-year.

sot_source.original: ...at móti sumri skyldi blóta í móti sigri.
sot_source.translation: ...at the start of summer there should be a sacrifice for victory.

historical_purpose: Sacrifice for victory (sigr) as the raiding and sailing season begins.
archaeological_context: Votive weapon deposits found in Danish bogs (e.g., Illerup Ådal) represent the practical application of Sigrblót.

required_tools.alt: A small knife, coin, or iron nail.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Open a window or door to face the East. State: 'Summer wakes, and the spear is raised.'
State one major goal for the summer. Pour the libation into the bowl as a 'deposit' on that victory.
Touch the iron piece to the liquid, then touch the iron to your forehead or your workstation.

sacrifice_standards.modern_equivalent: Spicy spirits (Odin's preference), iron items, or a committed 'victory project' (giving time).

adaptations.solo_practitioner: Focus on personal ambition and mental strength.
adaptations.apartment_safe: Use blue or green candles to represent the sky and new growth.
adaptations.sober_friendly: Strong black coffee or ginger tea (representing heat and energy).

sample_invocation: Hail to the Bringer of Victory! Summer is come. We seek the path, we seek the win. Odin, take this gift; Tyr, hold the law. Victory!

name: Vetrnætr (Winter Nights)

timing.historical_anchor: First day of Gormánuður (The first month of Winter)
timing.lunar_logic: Full Moon marking the end of harvest and onset of frost.

sot_source.original: ...að miðjum vetri til gróðrar, en hið þriðja að veturnóttum, það var til árs.
sot_source.translation: ...at mid-winter for growth, and the third at the winter nights for a good year.

historical_purpose: To welcome the winter and honor the Dísir (female guardians).
archaeological_context: Votive offerings of feminine jewelry and harvest grains found in household contexts support the domestic nature of the Dísablót.

required_tools.alt: A cloth for the altar or a ribbon.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Address the women of your line. 'To the mothers of the line, I welcome the cold with you.'
Pour a small amount of offering near the door or on a potted plant near the entry.
Three rounds: To the Dísir, to the Matriarchs, and to Peace/Plentiful Winter.

sacrifice_standards.historical: Autumn harvest products and female animals.
sacrifice_standards.modern_equivalent: Grains, dark beer, root vegetables, or hand-woven items.

adaptations.solo_practitioner: A quiet, meditative ritual focusing on home security and mental endurance.
adaptations.apartment_safe: A bowl of salt or a silver coin on the windowsill to represent the barrier.
adaptations.sober_friendly: Dark grape juice or apple cider.

sample_invocation: The gates of summer close. The Dísir watch the threshold. We welcome the dark, for the mothers are with us. Hail the Dísir!

name: Jól (Yule)

timing.lunar_logic: The first Full Moon following the first New Moon after the Winter Solstice.

sot_source.manuscript: Saga of Haakon the Good, Ch. 13-15
sot_source.original: Hann setti það í lögum að jart skulu hefja að sama tveim sem kristnir menn...
sot_source.translation: He made it law that Yule should begin at the same time as Christian men... but before it began on Hökunótt.

historical_purpose: Sacrifice for the growth (gróðrar) of the coming year.
archaeological_context: Evidence of mid-winter feasting in Chieftain halls (like Borg in Lofoten) shows the centralization of Jól as a political and spiritual bond.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Touch the evergreen to the candle/fire. 'Light returns, the wheel turns.'
Place your hand on the evergreen (representing the boar) and state one solemn oath for the year.
Pour the drink into the bowl. 'A gift for growth, a gift for the gods.'

sacrifice_standards.modern_equivalent: Pork roast, 'Boar Bread' (loaf shaped like a pig), or high-quality mead.

adaptations.solo_practitioner: Write your oaths in a journal and read them aloud to the fire/candle.
adaptations.apartment_safe: Use pine-scented incense if actual boughs are unavailable.
adaptations.sober_friendly: Honey-water (representing the sweetness of the return of the sun).

sample_invocation: In the depth of the dark, the light is born. Freyr, give us growth. Odin, give us wisdom. The year turns! Hail Jól!

name: Þorrablót

timing.historical_anchor: The beginning of the month of Þorri
timing.lunar_logic: Full Moon of the fourth winter month.

sot_source.original: Þeir kölluðu það Þorrablót... hann var blótaður til þess að snjór væri mikill.
sot_source.translation: They called it Thorrablot... he was sacrificed to so that there should be much snow (for travel).

historical_purpose: Sacrifice to personified Winter (Þorri) to ensure the frost remains manageable.
archaeological_context: Historical accounts describe the 'inviting' of winter inside, mirroring the personification seen in Saga narratives.

required_tools.alt: A pendant or a physical tool.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Step outside (barefoot for a moment). 'Hail to the Frost-King, we are unbowed.'
Hold the hammer over the food. 'Thor, ward this house from the biting cold.'
Eat the heartiest part of the meal first to show strength.

sacrifice_standards.modern_equivalent: Heavy ale, smoked meats/fish, or sturdy root vegetable stews.

adaptations.solo_practitioner: A celebration of survival and individual resilience.
adaptations.apartment_safe: Focus on the 'internal fire'—exercise or a hot bath to contrast the cold.
adaptations.sober_friendly: Root beer or ginger beer (spicy and grounding).

sample_invocation: Thor, Wielder of Mjölnir, stand at our door. The frost is deep, but our fire is hot. Ward the halls! Hail Thor!

name: Álfablót

timing.lunar_logic: Private moon cycle occurring after the public Dísablót.

sot_source.original: Úti er ekkert, inni er Álfablót.
sot_source.translation: There is nothing outside (for you); inside is the Elves' sacrifice.

historical_purpose: Private sacrifice to the Alfar (ancestors) for the luck of the home.
archaeological_context: Sacred groves (Alfar-haugar) and private household mounds suggest a lineage-based, private cult practice.

required_tools.alt: A jar of honey or honeycomb.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Lock the doors. This is strictly for the family/inner-circle.
Name your grandfathers and the men of the line. 'To the Alfar, we give the sweetness of the year.'
Place a bit of honey on bread. 'May the luck of the line stay within these walls.'

sacrifice_standards.historical: Livestock specifically for the family meal.
sacrifice_standards.modern_equivalent: Honey, fresh-baked bread, or high-quality butter.

adaptations.solo_practitioner: Reinforce the 'luck' of the individual dwelling; name your specific line or influences.
adaptations.apartment_safe: Turn off all electronics for 30 minutes to ensure total focus.

sample_invocation: The harvest is in, the doors are shut. To the Alfar who built the path: here is sweetness, here is thanks. Hail the Alfar!
